summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Gogs mirror settings endpoint allows changing the mirror address to an internal URL without local-address validation. After updating the address and triggering a mirror sync, the Gogs server initiates a TCP connection to the internal canary HTTP server, proving the SSRF bypass.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the Gogs server to make an HTTP connection to the internal canary listener at 127.0.0.1:8888 after the mirror address is changed via the settings endpoint and mirror sync is triggered.
actual_observed_result: The Gogs server was built from source, started with SQLite, and a mirror repository was created via migration with a valid external URL. The mirror address was then changed via the settings endpoint to http://127.0.0.1:8888/ssrf-proof-endpoint without any validation error. Triggering mirror sync caused the Gogs server to connect to the internal canary, which logged the incoming connection.
verification_method: An internal HTTP canary server listens on 127.0.0.1:8888 and logs all incoming connections to a file. After the exploit sequence completes, the verification script checks whether the canary log contains a connection record. This distinguishes genuine SSRF (server-initiated connection to internal address) from unrelated behavior such as startup failures or normal HTTP traffic.
evidence_produced_by_run: The internal canary HTTP server received a GET request from the Gogs server process after the mirror address was changed to an internal URL.
The migration endpoint correctly validates external URLs with netutil.IsBlockedLocalHostname, but the settings endpoint bypasses this check.
The mirror sync mechanism (git.IsURLAccessible and git remote update) initiated a connection to the attacker-specified internal address.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction builds Gogs from source which may take several minutes depending on network and CPU speed.
The initial mirror clone from the external URL (github.com/git/git.git) requires internet access.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
